The connection must meet the following requirements: ✑ Traffic must NOT traverse the public internet. ✑ Bandwidth must be guaranteed at 5 Gbps. ✑ Latency must be consistent and predictable. ✑ A financial SLA is required. Which Azure connectivity service should the bank use?
choices_text: ["Azure VPN Gateway (Site-to-Site)", "Azure VPN Gateway (Point-to-Site)", "Azure ExpressRoute", "Azure Virtual WAN"]

text: A company has a web application with the following traffic routing requirements: ✑ Requests to example.com/api must go to the API server farm. ✑ Requests to example.com/images must go to the image server farm. ✑ SSL/TLS must be terminated at the load balancer level. ✑ Protection against SQL injection attacks must be included. Which Azure service should they deploy?
choices_text: ["Azure Load Balancer with NSG rules", "Azure Traffic Manager", "Azure VPN Gateway", "Azure Application Gateway with WAF"]

text: A gaming company hosts a multiplayer game backend on 5 Azure VMs. Players connect via UDP. The company needs to distribute player connections across all 5 VMs to maximize performance. No HTTP inspection is needed. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure service should they use?
choices_text: ["Azure Application Gateway", "Azure Traffic Manager", "Azure Load Balancer", "Azure VPN Gateway"]

text: A company has deployed two Azure Virtual Networks in different regions: one in West Europe and one in East US. Development teams in both regions need to access shared resources in both VNets using private IP addresses, without sending traffic through the public internet. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which solution should they implement?
choices_text: ["Site-to-Site VPN between the two VNets", "Global VNet Peering", "Azure ExpressRoute", "Azure Load Balancer"]

text: A remote employee working from home needs to securely access resources in the company's Azure Virtual Network, including internal web servers and file shares. The connection must be secure and encrypted. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which VPN connection type should be configured?

A company has deployed Azure Storage accounts containing sensitive financial data. The security team requires that these storage accounts are only accessible from within the company's Azure Virtual Network using private IP addresses, and should not be reachable from the public internet at all. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure service should they configure?
choices_text: ["Azure Firewall", "Network Security Groups (NSG)", "Azure Private Link with Private Endpoints", "Azure VPN Gateway"]

text: A company's subnet in Azure hosts 20 web server VMs. The security team needs to create a rule that blocks all inbound SSH traffic (port 22) from the internet to this subnet, while allowing all other inbound traffic on port 443 (HTTPS). The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure feature should they use?
choices_text: ["Azure Firewall", "Azure DDoS Protection", "Network Security Group (NSG)", "Azure Application Gateway"]

text: A company is experiencing DDoS attacks on their public-facing Azure web application. The attacks have been growing in scale and the basic Azure DDoS protection is no longer sufficient. They need advanced mitigation with real-time telemetry, SLA guarantees, and detailed attack reports. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which solution should they implement?
choices_text: ["Azure DDoS Protection Basic (already enabled)", "Azure DDoS Protection Standard", "Azure Firewall Premium", "Azure Application Gateway WAF"]

text: A company hosts its DNS domain (contoso.com) with an external registrar. They want to move the management of DNS records for contoso.com to Azure so they can manage their infrastructure and DNS in one place, using Azure tools and RBAC. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure service should they use?
choices_text: ["Azure Private DNS", "Azure VPN Gateway", "Azure DNS (Public zone)", "Azure Traffic Manager"]

text: A company has deployed an internal HR application in Azure. The application servers are in a VNet and must be accessible from the company's on-premises Active Directory servers over a private connection. However, a recent audit revealed that the connection uses the public internet and is unencrypted. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which solution should they implement quickly?
choices_text: ["Azure ExpressRoute", "Azure VPN Gateway (Site-to-Site)", "Azure Application Gateway", "VNet Peering"]

text: A company is designing their Azure network security strategy. They have a production VNet and a development VNet. Developers should be able to connect their local laptops directly to the development VNet to test applications. What type of connection should they configure? The solution must meet the following requirements: Which connection type is most appropriate?

A company wants to protect internal Azure Virtual Network resources from threats originating outside the VNet, with the ability to filter traffic based on both domain names (FQDNs) and IP addresses, and log all traffic for compliance purposes. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure service should they use?
choices_text: ["Network Security Groups (NSG)", "Azure DDoS Protection", "Azure Firewall", "Azure Application Gateway"]

text: A company has two VNets in the same Azure region: a Hub VNet containing shared services (Active Directory, DNS) and a Spoke VNet containing workload VMs. The workload VMs need to access the shared services using private IP addresses without internet traffic. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which solution is most appropriate?
choices_text: ["Site-to-Site VPN between Hub and Spoke", "VNet Peering between Hub and Spoke", "ExpressRoute between Hub and Spoke", "Azure Load Balancer connecting the two VNets"]

text: A startup is building a multi-region web application. They need to direct users to the nearest Azure region based on geographic location to minimize latency. If one region becomes unavailable, traffic must automatically failover to another region. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure service should they use? ✅ End of scenario questions — Give this document to your trainer for correction. Your trainer will provide answer explanations and discuss common mistakes from this module.
choices_text: ["Azure Load Balancer", "Azure Application Gateway", "Azure Traffic Manager", "Azure VPN Gateway"]

text: What must be globally unique when creating an Azure Storage Account?
choices_text: ["The resource group name", "The storage account name", "The subscription ID", "The region name"]

text: Which Azure Storage service is best for storing large amounts of unstructured data such as images, videos, and backups?
Which Azure Storage service provides fully managed file shares accessible via the SMB protocol, ideal for replacing on-premises file servers?
Which Azure Storage service is used to store messages that can be accessed by multiple services in a decoupled architecture?
Which Azure Storage service provides high-performance block storage for use with Azure Virtual Machines?
choices_text: ["Azure Blob Storage", "Azure Files", "Azure Queues", "Azure Disks (Managed Disks)"]

text: What does LRS (Locally Redundant Storage) provide?
choices_text: ["Three copies of data across three different Azure regions", "Three synchronous copies of data within a single datacenter", "Six copies across two regions", "Two copies across two different datacenters in the same region"]

text: What does ZRS (Zone-Redundant Storage) provide?
choices_text: ["Three copies within the same datacenter", "Three synchronous copies across three Availability Zones in the primary region", "Six copies across two paired regions", "Copies in multiple countries"]

text: What does GRS (Geo-Redundant Storage) provide?
choices_text: ["Three copies within one datacenter", "Three copies across three AZs", "Six copies: three in the primary region and three in a paired secondary region", "Data only in two AZs"]

text: Which storage redundancy option provides the highest durability and protection against both zone and regional failures?
Which Blob Storage access tier is best for data that is rarely accessed and stored for at least 180 days?
Which Blob Storage access tier offers the lowest storage cost but the highest access cost?
What is Azure Migrate?
choices_text: ["A service to move data between storage tiers", "A unified platform to assess and migrate on-premises servers, databases, and web apps to Azure", "A VPN migration tool", "A DNS migration service"]

text: What is Azure Data Box?
choices_text: ["A cloud-based storage account type", "A physical device used to transfer large amounts of data to Azure when network transfer is impractical", "A software tool for migrating databases", "An Azure storage backup solution"]

text: Which storage service is most appropriate for storing backup data that needs to be retained for 7 years but accessed very rarely?
Which redundancy option replicates data to a secondary geographic region but does NOT allow reading from that secondary region by default?
What is the purpose of Azure Storage lifecycle management policies?
choices_text: ["To encrypt storage data", "To automatically transition blob data between access tiers or delete it based on age", "To manage RBAC on storage accounts", "To replicate data across regions"]

text: Which Azure storage redundancy option allows you to read data from the secondary region even when the primary region is healthy?
A company needs to migrate 500 TB of data to Azure. Their internet connection is slow. What should they use?
choices_text: ["Azure VPN Gateway", "Azure Blob Storage lifecycle", "Azure Data Box", "Azure Migrate"]

text: Which type of Azure Managed Disk offers the highest performance for I/O-intensive workloads?
What is a Shared Access Signature (SAS) in Azure Storage?
choices_text: ["A storage replication mechanism", "A URI that grants restricted access rights to Azure Storage resources without exposing the account key", "A type of storage redundancy", "A disk encryption method"]

text: Which TWO Azure Storage redundancy options replicate data to a secondary Azure region? (Choose 2)
Which TWO Azure Blob Storage tiers are correct? (Choose 2)
choices_text: ["Archive tier has the lowest storage cost", "Hot tier is designed for infrequently accessed data", "Cool tier is best for data accessed frequently", "Archive tier data can be read instantly without rehydration", "Hot tier is best for frequently accessed data"]

text: Which TWO Azure services are part of the Azure Migrate hub? (Choose 2)
choices_text: ["Azure Data Box", "Server Assessment tool", "Azure Functions", "Azure Migrate: Server Migration tool", "Azure Monitor"]

text: Which TWO statements about Azure Storage Accounts are correct? (Choose 2)
choices_text: ["The storage account name must be globally unique", "A storage account can store Blobs, Files, Queues, and Tables", "A single storage account can only contain one type of storage service", "Storage accounts are always replicated across regions by default", "Storage accounts don't support encryption"]

text: Which TWO factors affect the cost of Azure Storage? (Choose 2)
choices_text: ["The number of Azure VMs in the same region", "The amount of data stored", "The number of read and write operations", "The color scheme of the Azure Portal", "The age of the Azure subscription"]

text: Azure Blob Storage supports three access tiers: Hot, Cool, and Archive.
LRS (Locally Redundant Storage) replicates data across three different Azure regions.
Azure Data Box can be used to migrate large datasets to Azure when internet bandwidth is insufficient.
Azure Files shares can be mounted simultaneously on cloud and on-premises machines.
ZRS (Zone-Redundant Storage) protects data against an entire Azure region failure.

A hospital must retain patient scan images for a minimum of 7 years to comply with medical regulations. The images are accessed frequently during the first 6 months after creation, occasionally during months 7-12, and almost never after the first year. Storage costs must be minimized. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which combination of Blob access tiers and lifecycle management best addresses this?
choices_text: ["Store all images in Hot tier for 7 years", "Start in Hot, move to Cool after 30 days, move to Archive after 180 days", "Start in Cool, move to Archive after 30 days", "Store all images in Archive from day one"]

text: A media company stores video files for active production projects. These files are accessed multiple times per day by editors and rendering servers. The company wants the lowest possible access latency and is willing to pay more for storage to get faster access. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure Blob Storage access tier should they use?

A company is migrating 800 TB of data from their on-premises datacenter to Azure. Their internet connection is 1 Gbps. The IT manager calculates that transferring 800 TB over 1 Gbps would take approximately 74 days. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure service should they use?
choices_text: ["Azure ExpressRoute (10 Gbps circuit)", "Azure Data Box Heavy", "AzCopy over the existing internet connection", "Azure Migrate with agent-based replication"]

text: A company needs to replace its aging on-premises Windows file server. The new solution must: ✑ Be accessible from Windows and Linux machines simultaneously. ✑ Use the SMB 3.0 protocol. ✑ Allow files to be mapped as a network drive on Windows. ✑ Require no server management. Which Azure service should they use?
choices_text: ["Azure Blob Storage", "Azure Queue Storage", "Azure Table Storage", "Azure Files"]

text: A company is building a distributed order processing system. When a customer places an order, a message is generated and must be stored temporarily until the fulfillment service picks it up and processes it. The two services operate independently and asynchronously. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure Storage service should they use?
choices_text: ["Azure Blob Storage", "Azure Queue Storage", "Azure Files", "Azure Table Storage"]

text: A company is selecting a redundancy option for Azure Storage. Their primary concern is protection against a complete Azure regional outage. They also need to read data from the secondary region at any time without waiting for a failover event. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which redundancy option should they choose?

A startup is building a new application and needs to choose an Azure Storage redundancy option. The data is non-critical (development logs). Cost must be absolutely minimized. High durability is not a priority. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which redundancy option should they choose?

A company needs to grant a third-party analytics partner temporary read-only access to a specific Azure Blob container containing sales data. The access should expire automatically after 48 hours. The company's storage account keys must not be shared. The solution must meet the following requirements: What should they provide to the partner?
choices_text: ["The storage account access key", "A Shared Access Signature (SAS) token with read permissions and 48-hour expiry", "An Azure AD guest user account with Contributor role", "Public access enabled on the container"]

text: A company has on-premises VMware virtual machines they want to migrate to Azure. Before migrating, they need to assess which VMs are ready for Azure, what size VMs they should use in Azure, and what the estimated monthly Azure cost would be. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure service provides all of these capabilities?
choices_text: ["Azure Data Box", "Azure Backup", "Azure Site Recovery", "Azure Migrate"]

text: A company wants to automatically transition Azure Blob data through storage tiers based on age to minimize costs. Blobs should move from Hot to Cool after 30 days of no access, and be deleted entirely after 365 days. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure feature should they configure?
choices_text: ["Azure Policy", "Azure Blob Lifecycle Management policies", "Azure Automation runbooks", "Azure Monitor with action groups"]

text: A company is choosing between GRS and GZRS for their critical business data. Their primary concern is maximum protection against both datacenter-level failures within their region AND complete regional outages. Budget is secondary. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which option provides the highest durability?

A company stores structured NoSQL data in key-value pairs for their IoT device telemetry application. The data does not require complex relational queries. They need a cost-effective, scalable storage solution. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure Storage service is most appropriate?
choices_text: ["Azure SQL Database", "Azure Blob Storage", "Azure Table Storage", "Azure Files"]

text: A legal firm must retain all contract documents for 10 years per regulatory requirements. Documents older than 2 years are almost never accessed. The firm wants to minimize storage costs for these archived documents while keeping them accessible within a few hours if needed. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure Blob Storage access tier best meets these requirements?

A company needs to provide high-performance shared storage for 3 Azure Virtual Machines running a clustered database application. The storage must provide consistent low latency and high IOPS. The storage must be accessible simultaneously by all 3 VMs. The solution must meet the following requirements: Which Azure storage solution should they use?
choices_text: ["Azure Blob Storage", "Azure Queue Storage", "Azure Files (Premium tier with NFS)", "Azure Archive Storage"]

text: A company is migrating to Azure and needs to choose the right redundancy for different workloads: mission-critical banking transactions, standard employee documents, and non-critical development logs. They must match the right option to each workload. Which redundancy option is most appropriate for mission-critical banking transaction data requiring protection against regional disasters? ✅ End of scenario questions — Give this document to your trainer for correction. Your trainer will provide answer explanations and discuss common mistakes from this module.
choices_text: ["LRS \u2014 three copies in one datacenter", "ZRS \u2014 three copies across availability zones", "GRS \u2014 copies in two regions", "GZRS \u2014 zone-redundant in primary region + geo-replication to secondary region"]
